title: strychnos angolensis
intro: Strychnos angolensis, also known as the Angola poison nut, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Strychnos angolensis include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Strychnos angolensis can be made into infusions, tinctures, or decoctions, and are often used in combination with other plants to enhance their therapeutic effects.
